data.body:
Goal:
Replace the ~3000-line `foolery setup` bash wizard with a single agent-driven skill. Any frontier agent (Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, Copilot) invokes the skill, detects CLIs on PATH, asks at most four questions, and writes a validated ~/.config/foolery/settings.toml + ~/.config/foolery/registry.json. The skill leans on the already-shipped `foolery config schema` and `foolery config validate` commands (knot e33e) as the stable contract for the target file.

This knot ships ONLY the skill file. Deleting `scripts/setup.sh` and rerouting the launcher are scoped as separate follow-on knots so the skill can be validated in the wild first.

Verification:
1. `.claude/skills/foolery-configure/SKILL.md` exists with YAML frontmatter matching the existing convention in `.claude/skills/ship-release/SKILL.md`.
2. The skill body references `foolery config schema` and `foolery config validate` verbatim so the agent has a machine-readable spec.
3. The skill instructs the agent to write to `~/.config/foolery/settings.toml` and `~/.config/foolery/registry.json` atomically (tempfile + mv).
4. The skill preserves existing configuration by default — never drops registered agents or custom `[scopeRefinement].prompt`.
5. The skill enumerates the four allowed questions and nothing more.
6. The skill caps itself at ~120 lines so it stays readable.
7. Quality gates green: `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, `bun run build`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT delete `scripts/setup.sh`, `scripts/agent-wizard.sh`, `scripts/dispatch-pools.sh`, `scripts/model-picker.sh`, or `scripts/toml-reader.sh` — follow-on knot.
- Do NOT modify `scripts/install.sh` `setup_cmd` — follow-on knot.
- Agent-agnostic tone: no Claude-only tool names in the body; use plain shell/file verbs.
- Follow the "fail loudly, never silently" rule: skill must tell the agent to ask the user when a value is missing, never to pick a default.
data.title: Add foolery-configure skill: agent-driven settings.toml generation
